title: 🟢 Two Pointers — Active Recall Skeletons
oneSentence: Fill in the blank active recall templates for Two Pointer problems.

twoSumII.title: Two Sum II — Active Recall
twoSumII.story: Opposite ends. Too small? Move left up. Too big? Move right down.
template: public static int[] solve(int[] nums, int target) {
// STEP 1 — INIT: Place pointers at opposite ends of the sorted array
// STEP 2 — LOOP: Narrow inward until the pointers meet
// STEP 3 — CHECK: Compare sum against target and decide which pointer to move
return new int[]{[[left + 1]], [[right + 1]]}; // match → return 1-indexed answer
[[left++]];   // sum too small → move left up to increase
[[right--]];  // sum too big → move right down to decrease
// STEP 4 — RETURN: Fallback (problem guarantees a solution)

palindrome.title: Valid Palindrome — Active Recall
palindrome.story: Skip non-alphanumeric junk inside the loop, compare lowercase, move both inwards.
template: // STEP 1 — INIT: Place pointers at both ends of the string
// STEP 2 — LOOP: Walk inward until the two friends meet
// STEP 3 — SKIP: Jump over non-alphanumeric junk on both sides
// STEP 4 — CHECK: Compare the two valid characters (case-insensitive)
// STEP 5 — MOVE: Both matched → step inward
// STEP 6 — RETURN: All matched → IS a palindrome

threeSum.title: 3Sum — Active Recall
threeSum.story: Sort first! Loop anchor i, do TwoSumII on left (i+1) and right (end), skip duplicate anchors.
template: // STEP 1 — INIT: Sort the array (required for two pointers to work)
// STEP 2 — LOOP: Pick each element as the "anchor"
// STEP 3 — PRUNE: Early exit & skip duplicate anchors
if ([[nums[i] > 0]]) [[break]];                           // impossible to sum to 0 → stop
if (i > 0 && [[nums[i] == nums[i - 1]]]) [[continue]];    // same anchor → skip duplicate
// STEP 4 — INIT POINTERS: Set up TwoSumII on the remaining subarray
// STEP 5 — INNER LOOP: TwoSumII to find pairs that cover the anchor's debt
// STEP 6 — CHECK: Compare sum and decide which pointer to move
while (left < right && [[nums[left] == nums[left - 1]]]) [[left++]];   // skip dup left
while (left < right && [[nums[right] == nums[right + 1]]]) [[right--]]; // skip dup right
[[left++]];   // sum too small → need bigger, move left up
[[right--]];  // sum too big → need smaller, move right down

trappingRain.title: Trapping Rain Water — Active Recall
trappingRain.story: Bathtub walls. Process shorter wall first. If wall >= wallMax -> update max, else trap water.
template: // STEP 1 — INIT: Place pointers at both ends, track max heights from each side
// STEP 2 — LOOP: Walk inwards until pointers meet
// STEP 3 — CHECK: Which side is the bottleneck? Process that side.
// Left side is shorter → it's the bottleneck
// STEP 4a — UPDATE: Is this wall taller than any we've seen on the left?
leftMax = [[height[left]]]; // new tallest wall → update boundary
water += [[leftMax - height[left]]]; // lower than boundary → water trapped!
// STEP 5a — MOVE: Done with this position → move inward
// Right side is shorter → it's the bottleneck
// STEP 4b — UPDATE: Is this wall taller than any we've seen on the right?
rightMax = [[height[right]]]; // new tallest wall → update boundary
water += [[rightMax - height[right]]]; // lower than boundary → water trapped!
// STEP 5b — MOVE: Done with this position → move inward

linkedListCycle.title: Linked List Cycle — Active Recall
linkedListCycle.story: Tortoise (1 step) and Hare (2 steps) collide on a circular track if cycle exists.
template: // STEP 1 — INIT: Both pointers start at the head node
// STEP 2 — LOOP: Walk through the list as long as the fast pointer can move 2 steps
// STEP 3 — MOVE: Slow moves 1 step, Fast moves 2 steps
// STEP 4 — CHECK: If they meet at the same node, a cycle is detected!
// STEP 5 — RETURN: Fast pointer reached the end of the list → no cycle
